disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The loss of consciousness came on while sitting and watching a graphic surgical scene on television, in a warm room
trigger_question_examples: What were you doing right before you passed out?
Was there anything that seemed to set it off?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I was just sitting watching a surgery scene on TV in a warm room — my stomach flipped and it went from there.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Before losing consciousness there was nausea, heavy sweating, a hot flushed feeling, tunnel vision and ringing in the ears
trigger_question_examples: Did you have any warning before you passed out?
How did you feel in the seconds beforehand?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah — I felt sick and really sweaty and hot, my vision tunnelled in and my ears were ringing, and then I was gone.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Consciousness returned rapidly — within about 20 seconds — once the patient was lying flat
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It was really quick — my partner says I was out for under half a minute and I came to almost as soon as I was flat on my back.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The patient fell during the event, sinking onto carpet; no witnessed head strike, laceration or bleeding, but a knock cannot be excluded
trigger_question_examples: Did you hit your head when you fell?
Any cuts, bumps or bleeding from the fall?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I kind of slumped down onto the carpet. My partner didn't see me crack my head and there's no cut or lump, but honestly I don't remember the fall itself.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No jerking of the limbs, no tongue biting, no loss of bladder or bowel control, and no confusion or drowsiness after coming round
trigger_question_examples: Did anyone see any shaking or jerking?
Did you bite your tongue or wet yourself, and were you confused afterwards?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No shaking that my partner saw, I didn't bite my tongue or lose control of anything, and I knew exactly where I was as soon as I woke up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No chest pain, no palpitations, and no sense the heart was racing or pounding before the event
trigger_question_examples: Any chest pain or a racing/pounding heart before you went?
Did it come completely out of the blue with no warning?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No chest pain and I didn't feel my heart racing — it was the sick, sweaty, tunnel-vision feeling that came first, not a sudden drop.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No exertion at the time (was seated), and no personal or family history of sudden cardiac death, unexplained collapse or inherited heart problems volunteered
trigger_question_examples: Were you exercising or exerting yourself when it happened?
Has anyone in your family died suddenly or young, or had a heart-rhythm problem?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I was just sitting on the sofa, not exerting myself. No, no one in my family has dropped dead suddenly or had that sort of heart problem that I know of.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: First-ever episode of loss of consciousness; currently feels drained and washed-out but otherwise well
trigger_question_examples: How do you feel right now?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Never happened before, this is the first time. I feel completely wiped out but otherwise okay.
